core_claim: Judgeability must be resolved before polarity. The architecture separates whether a claim can be evaluated from how it should be evaluated. No polarity signal may rescue a claim blocked by an upstream judgeability axis.

final_order.name: Hard Core Validity
final_order.description: Validates temporal and source-frame integrity. Cannot be overridden by any downstream axis.

final_order.name: Coverage / Entailment
final_order.description: Determines whether evidence semantically covers the claim domain. Distinct from temporal frame mismatch.

final_order.name: Residual Adjudication
final_order.description: Handles partially covered, ambiguous, or conflicting evidence. No global preservation rescue. No double penalty.

final_order.name: ANI-style Epistemic Diagnosis
final_order.description: Measures model uncertainty and epistemic state. Diagnostic readout only — not a primary decision axis.

final_order.name: Polarity Layer
final_order.description: Determines support or refutation direction, only if upstream judgeability axes passed.

final_order.name: Final Composer
final_order.description: Assembles the final label (Supported / Refuted / NEI) from all upstream signals. Enforces the Single Owner Rule.

invariants: Cannot be overridden by any downstream axis
Temporal frame mismatch is a Hard Core concern, not a Coverage concern
If Hard Core fires, Final Composer must emit NEI; polarity is not consulted
Coverage/Entailment is strictly distinct from temporal frame mismatch
Low coverage triggers NEI before polarity is computed
Must not be conflated with temporal residual preservation
No global preservation rescue: cannot rescue claims blocked by Hard Core
No double penalty: claims already penalized by Hard Core receive no additional residual penalty
Operates only over evidence not resolved by Coverage/Entailment
ANI is a diagnostic readout, not a primary decision axis
ANI output is available to Final Composer but cannot unilaterally change a verdict
Used for calibration, confidence scoring, and post-hoc analysis only
Only receives claims confirmed judgeable by upstream axes
Outputs: supports, refutes, or abstains if upstream blocked
Enforces Single Owner Rule: each label has exactly one owning axis
No two axes may claim the same label for conflicting reasons
Emits final prediction with attached confidence

single_owner_rule.definition: Each verdict outcome (Supported, Refuted, NEI) is owned by exactly one primary axis. Overlapping ownership is a spec violation.

forbidden_behaviors.description: No downstream axis (Coverage/Entailment, ANI, Polarity) may override or reverse a Hard Core block.
Temporal preservation signals cannot rescue claims invalidated by Hard Core. Preservation signals are local to Residual Adjudication and must not propagate upward.
If Hard Core fires a penalty for a claim, downstream axes must not apply an independent penalty for the same root cause.
Coverage/Entailment must not handle temporal or source-frame invalidity. That is exclusively a Hard Core concern.
ANI readout must not serve as a primary decision input. It is diagnostic only.
Polarity must not be computed for claims not confirmed judgeable by upstream axes.

stage30e_scope.description: Stage30-E is a minimal, targeted implementation scoped exclusively to temporal residual preservation-aware cap logic.

implements.name: Temporal residual preservation-aware cap
implements.description: Caps the effective temporal mismatch penalty using a temporal preservation probability signal.

implements.name: Reuse of Stage30-D temporal mismatch risk
implements.description: Reads temporal_mismatch_fused_prob from Stage30-D output; does not recompute it.

implements.name: New temporal preservation signal
implements.description: Introduces temporal_preservation_prob as a new signal representing how well the preserved temporal context aligns with the claim window.

implements.name: Effective penalty formula
implements.description: Computes the effective temporal mismatch penalty after preservation discounting.

forbidden_in_stage30e.description: Stage30-E must not implement the Coverage/Entailment axis. Reserved for a future stage.
Stage30-E must not implement full ANI-style epistemic readout. Reserved for a future stage.
Stage30-E must not implement the full residual vector or Residual Adjudication axis. Reserved for a future stage.
Stage30-E must not refactor or restructure the Hard Core Validity module. Its structure is fixed in this stage.
Stage30-E must not use preservation signals to rescue claims blocked by Hard Core.
Stage30-E must not use Stage30-D cap output directly as a final candidate score. It must apply the effective penalty formula.

non_goals: Full residual vector and Residual Adjudication (reserved for Stage32)
Full ANI-style epistemic readout integration (reserved for Stage33)
Direct Stage30-D cap passthrough as final output

allowed_future_stages.scope: Full residual vector and Residual Adjudication axis
Final Composer multi-axis fusion and calibration
